steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $x^{3}+14x^{2}-8x+40$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute the 5
steps.content:
Multiply 5 by each term inside the second parentheses:
$$5(x^2+8) = 5x^2 + 40$$

steps.title: Remove parentheses and group like terms
steps.content:
Rewrite the full expression:
$$x^3 + 9x^2 - 8x + 5x^2 + 40$$
Collect the $x^2$ terms: $9x^2 + 5x^2 = 14x^2$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $x^{3}+14x^{2}-8x+40$: Distributing 5 gives $5x^2+40$; combining $9x^2+5x^2=14x^2$ produces this expression. ✓
  • Choice B — $x^{3}+9x^{2}-8x+40$: Keeps $9x^2$ unchanged, forgetting to add the $5x^2$ from distributing. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{3}+5x^{2}-8x+40$: Replaces $9x^2$ with $5x^2$ instead of adding them. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{3}+8x^{2}-8x+40$: $8x^2$ cannot result from any combination of the terms present. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
